title: thymus neurophyllus
intro: Thymus neurophyllus, also known as Creeping Thyme, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its tiny leaves and pink, white, or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Thymus neurophyllus have been shown to have a range of health benefits, including reducing stress, improving cognitive function, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Thymus neurophyllus can be used in teas, tinctures, and essential oils, allowing for various applications, including skincare and aromatherapy.
